policy: Component-crop drift is the gated metric for production grades. Whole-screen drift is informational only: cross-platform font rasterization, GPU paths, and OS composition differ, so a high whole-screen change ratio does not by itself demote a component grade. Whole-screen values below are read from the checked-in pixel-diff artifacts and must match them exactly.

componentCropDrift.metric: component-evidence crop grade and per-component thresholds
componentCropDrift.note: Supported Ring 0 basic input controls are usable; planned rich-input rows remain not-rendered.

wholeScreenDrift.note: Superseded by component evidence; cross-platform rasterization difference.

componentCropDrift.metric: component-evidence crop grade and per-component thresholds
componentCropDrift.note: Supported command surfaces and the partial flyout/menu subset are usable; planned controls remain not-rendered.

wholeScreenDrift.note: Superseded by component evidence; cross-platform rasterization difference.

componentCropDrift.metric: component-evidence crop grade and per-component thresholds
componentCropDrift.note: Ring 0 layout/resource regions are usable; media, web, ink, materials, and advanced visuals remain excluded or planned.

wholeScreenDrift.note: Superseded by component evidence; cross-platform rasterization difference.

componentCropDrift.metric: component-evidence crop grade and per-component thresholds
componentCropDrift.note: Workbench navigation, list/detail, command, and status subset is usable; adaptive behavior remains partial.

wholeScreenDrift.note: Historical whole-image comparison; superseded by component evidence for the documented workbench subset.
